author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter I. 19–33.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have very often heard, holy brethren, and you know well, that John the Baptist, in proportion as he was greater than those born of women, and was more humble in his acknowledgment of the Lord, obtained the grace of being the friend of the Bridegroom; zealous for the Bridegroom, not for himself; not seeking his own honor, but that of his Judge, whom as a herald he preceded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, to the prophets who went before, it was granted to predict concerning Christ; but to this man, to point Him out with the finger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as Christ was unknown by those who did not believe the prophets before He came, He remained unknown to them even when present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had come humbly and concealed from the first; the more concealed in proportion as He was more humble: but the people, despising in their pride the humility of God, crucified their Saviour, and made Him their condemner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But will not He who at first came con cealed, because humble, come again manifested, because exalted?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have just listened to the Psalm: “God shall come manifestly, and our God shall not keep silence.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was silent that He might be judged, He will not be silent when He begins to judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It would not have been said, “He will come manifestly,” unless at first He had come concealed; nor would it have been said, “He shall not keep silence,” unless He had first kept silence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How was He silent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Interrogate Isaiah: “He was brought as a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before his shearer was dumb, so He opened not His mouth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But He shall come manifestly, and shall not keep silence.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what manner “manifestly”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “A fire shall go before Him, and round about Him a strong tempest.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That tempest has to carry away all the chaff from the floor, which is now being threshed; and the fire has to burn what the tempest carries away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He is silent; silent in judgment, but not silent in precept.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if Christ is silent, what is the purpose of these Gospels? what the purpose of the voices of the apostles, what of the canticles of the Psalms, what of the declarations of the prophets?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In all these Christ is not silent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now He is silent in not taking vengeance: He is not silent in not giving warning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He will come in glory to take vengeance, and will manifest Himself even to all who do not believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, because when present He was concealed, it behoved that He should be despised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For unless He had been despised, He would not have been crucified; if He had not been crucified, He would not have shed His blood—the price by which He redeemed us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that He might give a price for us, He was crucified; that He might be crucified, He was despised; that He might be despised, He appeared in humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet because He appeared as it were in the night, in a mortal body, He lighted for Himself a lamp by which He might be seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That lamp was John, concerning whom you lately heard many things: and the present passage of the evangelist contains the words of John; in the first place, and it is the chief point, his confession that he was not the Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But so great was the excellence of John, that men might have believed him to be the Christ: and in this he gave a proof of his humility, that he said he was not when he might have been believed to have been the Christ; therefore, “This is the testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites to him from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But they would not have sent unless they had been moved by the excellence of his authority who ventured to baptize.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he confessed, and denied not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did he confess?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And he confessed, I am not the Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they asked him, What then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou Elias?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they knew that Elias was to precede Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to no Jew was the name of Christ unknown.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They did not think that he was the Christ; but they did not think that Christ would not come at all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When they were hoping that He would come, they were offended at Him when He was present, and stumbled at Him as on a low stone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was as yet a small stone, already indeed cut out of the mountain without hands; as saith Daniel the prophet, that he saw a stone cut out of the mountain without hands.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And that stone,” saith he “grew and became a great mountain and filled the whole face of the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark then, my beloved brethren, what I say: Christ, before the Jews, was already cut out from the mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The prophet wishes that by the mountain should be understood the Jewish kingdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the kingdom of the Jews had not filled the whole face of the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The stone was cut out from thence, because from thence was the Lord born on His advent among men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore without hands?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because without the cooperation of man did the Virgin bear Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now then was that stone cut out without hands before the eyes of the Jews; but it was humble.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not without reason; because not yet had that stone increased and filled the whole earth: that He showed in His kingdom, which is the Church, with which He has filled the whole face of the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because then it had not yet increased, they stumbled at Him as at a stone: and that happened in them which is written, “Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever that stone shall fall, it will grind them to powder.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At first they fell upon Him lowly: as the lofty One He shall come upon them; but that He may grind them to powder when He comes in His exaltation, He first broke them in His lowliness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They stumbled at Him, and were broken; they were not ground, but broken: He will come exalted and will grind them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews were to be pardoned because they stumbled at a stone which had not yet increased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of persons are those who stumble at the mountain itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already you know who they are of whom I speak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who deny the Church diffused through the whole world, do not stumble at the lowly stone, but at the mountain itself: because this the stone became as it grew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The blind Jews did not see the lowly stone: but how great blindness not to see the mountain!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw Him then lowly, and did not know Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was pointed out to them by a lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the first place he, than whom no greater had arisen of those born of women, said, “I am not the Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was said to him, “Art thou Elias?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He answered, I am not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ sends Elias before Him: and he said, “I am not,” and occasioned a question for us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is to be feared lest men, insufficiently understanding, think that John contradicted what Christ said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in a certain place, when the Lord Jesus Christ said certain things in the Gospel regarding Himself, His disciples answered Him: “How then say the scribes,” that is, those skilled in the law, “that Elias must first come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord said, “Elias is already come, and they have done unto him what they listed;” and, if you wish to know, John the Baptist is he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus Christ said, “Elias is already come, and John the Baptist” is he; but John, being interrogated, confessed that he was not Elias, in the same manner that he confessed that he was not Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as his confession that he was not Christ was true, so was his confession that he was not Elias.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then shall we compare the words of the herald with the words of the Judge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought that the herald speaks falsehood; for that which he speaks he hears from the Judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then did he say, “I am not Elias;” and the Lord, “He is Elias”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the Lord Jesus Christ wished in him to prefigure His own advent, and to say that John was in the spirit of Elias.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what John was to the first advent, that will Elias be to the second advent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As there are two advents of the Judge, so are there two heralds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Judge indeed was the same, but the heralds two, but not two judges.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was needful that in the first instance the Judge should come to be judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He sent before Him His first herald; He called him Elias, because Elias will be in the second advent what John was in the first.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For mark, beloved brethren, how true it is what I say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When John was conceived, or rather when he was born, the Holy Spirit prophesied that this would be fulfilled in him: “And he shall be,” he said, “the forerunner of the Highest, in the spirit and power of Elias.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What signifieth “in the spirit and power of Elias”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same Holy Spirit in the room of Elias.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore in room of Elias?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because what Elias will be to the second, that John was to the first advent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly therefore, speaking literally, did John reply.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord spoke figuratively, “Elias, the same is John:” but he, as I have said, spoke literally when he said, “I am not Elias.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither did John speak falsely, nor did the Lord speak falsely; neither was the word of the herald nor of the Judge false, if only thou understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who shall understand?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who shall have imitated the lowliness of the herald, and shall have acknowledged the loftiness of the Judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For nothing was more lowly than the herald.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, in nothing had John greater merit than in this humility, inasmuch as when he was able to deceive men, and to be thought Christ, and to have been received in the place of Christ (for so great were his grace and his excellency), nevertheless he openly confessed and said, “I am not the Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou Elias?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he had said I am Elias, it would have been as if Christ were already coming in His second advent to judge, not in His first to be judged.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if saying, Elias is yet to come, “I am not,” said he, “Elias.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But give heed to the lowly One before whom John came, that you may not feel the lofty One before whom Elias came.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus also did the Lord complete the saying: “John the Baptist is he which is to come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came as a figure of that in which Elias is to come in his own person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then Elias will in his own proper person be Elias, now in similitude he was John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now John in his own proper person is John, in similitude Elias.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The two heralds gave to each other their similitudes, and kept their own proper persons; but the Judge is one Lord, whether preceded by this herald or by that.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they asked him, What then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Art thou Elias?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he said, No. And they said unto him, Art thou a prophet? and he answered, No!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They said therefore unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sayest thou of thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith, I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That said Isaiah.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This prophecy was fulfilled in John, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Crying what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight the paths of our God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would it not have seemed to you that a herald would have cried, “Go away, make room.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Instead of the herald’s cry “Go away,” John says “Come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The herald makes men stand back from the judge; to the Judge John calls.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yes, indeed, John calls men to the lowly One, that they may not experience what He will be as the exalted Judge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Isaiah.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not say, I am John, I am Elias, I am a prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what did he say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This I am called, “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare the way for the Lord: I am the prophecy itself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And they which were sent were of the Pharisees,” that is, of the chief men among the Jews; “and they asked him and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not the Christ, nor Elias, nor a prophet?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if it seemed to them audacity to baptize, as if they meant to inquire, in what character baptizest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We ask whether thou art the Christ; thou sayest that thou art not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We ask whether thou perchance art His precursor, for we know that before the advent of Christ, Elias will come; thou answerest that thou art not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We ask, if perchance thou art some herald come long before, that is, a prophet, and hast received that power, and thou sayest that thou art not a prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And John was not a prophet; he was greater than a prophet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord gave such testimony concerning him: “What went ye out into the wilderness to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A reed shaken with the wind?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of course implying that he was not shaken by the wind; because John was not such an one as is moved by the wind; for he who is moved by the wind is blown upon by every seductive blast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But what went ye out for to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A man clothed in soft raiment?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John was clothed in rough garments; that is, his tunic was of camel’s hair.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, they who are clothed in soft raiment are in kings’ houses.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You did not then go out to see a man clothed in soft raiment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But what went ye out for to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yea, I say unto you, one greater than a prophet is here;” for the prophets prophesied of Christ a long time before, John pointed Him out as present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not the Christ, nor Elias, nor a prophet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water; but there standeth One among you whom ye know not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, very truly, He was not seen, being humble, and therefore was the lamp lighted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe how John gives place, who might have been accounted other than he was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He it is who cometh after me, who is made before me” (that is, as we have already said, is “preferred before me”), whose shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How greatly did he humble himself!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And therefore he was greatly lifted up; for he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, holy brethren, you ought to note that if John so humbled himself as to say, “I am not worthy to unloose His shoe-latchet,” what need they have to be humbled who say, “We baptize; what we give is ours, and what is ours is holy.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said, Not I, but He; they say, We.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John is not worthy to unloose His shoe’s latchet; and if he had said he was worthy, how humble would he still have been!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if he had said he was worthy, and had spoken thus, “He came after me who is made before me, the latchet of whose shoe I am only worthy to unloose,” he would have greatly humbled himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when he says that he is not worthy even to do this, truly was he full of the Holy Spirit, who in such fashion as a servant acknowledged his Lord, and merited to be made a friend instead of a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “These things were done in Bethany, beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The next day John saw Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God; behold Him who taketh away the sin of the world!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no one so arrogate to himself as to say that he taketh away the sin of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed now to the proud men at whom John pointed the finger.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The heretics were not yet born, but already were they pointed out; against them he then cried from the river, against whom he now cries from the Gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Jesus comes, and what says he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold the Lamb of God!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If to be innocent is to be a lamb, then John was a lamb, for was not he innocent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who is innocent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To what extent innocent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All come from that branch and shoot, concerning which David sings, even with groanings, “Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Alone, then, was He, the Lamb who came, not so.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was not conceived in iniquity, because not conceived of mortality; nor did His mother conceive Him in sin, whom the Virgin conceived, whom the Virgin brought forth; because by faith she conceived, and by faith received Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “Behold the Lamb of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is not a branch derived from Adam: flesh only did he derive from Adam, Adam’s sin He did not assume.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who took not upon Him sin from our lump, He it is who taketh away our sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sin of the world!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know that certain men say sometimes, We take away sin from men, we who are holy; for if he be not holy who baptizeth, how taketh he away the sin of another, when he is a man himself full of sin?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In opposition to these disputations, let us not speak our own words, let us read what John says: “Behold the Lamb of God; behold Him who taketh away the sin of the world!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let there not be presumptuous confidence of men upon men: let not the sparrow flee to the mountains, but let it trust in the Lord; and if it lift its eyes to the mountains, from whence cometh aid to it, let it understand that its aid is from the Lord who made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So great is the excellence of John, that to him it is said, “Art thou the Christ?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says, No. Art thou Elias?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says, No. Art thou a prophet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says, No. Wherefore then dost thou baptize?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold the Lamb of God; behold Him who taketh away the sin of the world!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is He of whom I spake, After me cometh a Man who was made before me; for He was before me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Cometh after me,” because He was born later; “was made before me,” because preferred before me; “He was before me,” because, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And I knew Him not,” he said; “but that He might be made manifest to Israel, therefore came I baptizing with water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and abiding upon Him, the same is He who baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed for a little, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When did John learn Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he was sent to baptize with water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They asked, Wherefore?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He might be made manifest to Israel, he said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what profit was the baptism of John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, if it had profited in any respect, it would have remained now, and men would have been baptized with the baptism of John, and thus have come to the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what saith he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That He might be made manifest to Israel,”—that is, to Israel itself, to the people Israel, so that Christ might be made manifest to it,—therefore he came baptizing with water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John received the ministry of baptism, that by the water of repentance he might prepare the way for the Lord, not being himself the Lord; but where the Lord was known, it was superfluous to prepare for Him the way, for to those who knew Him He became Himself the way; therefore the baptism of John did not last long.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how was the Lord pointed out?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lowly, that John might so receive a baptism in which the Lord Himself should be baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And was it needful for the Lord to be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I instantly reply to any one who asks this question: Was it needful for the Lord to be born?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it needful for the Lord to be crucified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it needful for the Lord to die?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it needful for the Lord to be buried?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He undertook for us so great humiliation, might He not also receive baptism?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what profit was there that he received the baptism of a servant?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That thou mightest not disdain to receive the baptism of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, beloved brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certain catechumens were to arise in the Church of higher grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It sometimes comes to pass that you see a catechumen who practises continence, bids farewell to the world, renounces all his possessions, distributing them to the poor; and although but a catechumen, instructed in the saving doctrine better, perhaps, than many of the faithful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is to be feared regarding such an one that he may say to himself about holy baptism, whereby sins are remitted, What more shall I receive?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I am better than this faithful man, and this,—having in his mind those among the faithful who are either married, or who are perhaps ignorant, or who keep possession of their property, while he has given his to the poor,—and considering himself better than those who have been already baptized, he deigns not to come to baptism, saying, Am I to receive what this man has, and this thinking of persons whom he despises, and, as it were, considers it an indignity to receive that which inferiors have received, because he appears to himself to be already better than they; and, nevertheless, all his sins are upon him, and without coming to saving baptism, wherein all sins are remitted, he cannot, with all his excellence, enter into the kingdom of heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, in order to invite such excellence to his baptism, that sins might be remitted, Himself came to the baptism of His servant; and although He had no sin to be remitted, nor was there anything in Him that needed to be washed, He received baptism from a servant; and by so doing, addressed Himself to the son carrying himself proudly, and exalting himself, and disdaining, perhaps, to receive along with the ignorant that from which salvation comes to him, and said to him: How dost thou extend thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How dost thou exalt thyself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How great is thy excellence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How great is thy grace?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can it be greater than mine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I come to the servant, dost thou disdain to come to the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If I have received the baptism of the servant, dost thou disdain to be baptized by the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that you may know, my brethren, that not from a necessity of any chain of sin did the Lord come to this John, as the other evangelists say when the Lord came to him to be baptized, John himself said, “Comest Thou to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did He reply to him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Suffer it to be so now: let all righteousness be fulfilled?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth this, “let all righteousness be fulfilled”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I came to die for men, have I not to be baptized for men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth “let all righteousness be fulfilled”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let all humility be fulfilled.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not He to accept baptism from a good servant who accepted suffering at the hands of evil servants?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed then.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord being baptized, if John for this end baptized, that by means of his baptism the Lord might manifest His humility, should no one else have been baptized with the baptism of John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But many were baptized with the baptism of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord was baptized with the baptism of John, the baptism of John ceased.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John was forthwith cast into prison.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Afterwards we do not find that any one is baptized with that baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, John came baptizing for this end, that the humility of the Lord might be made manifest to us, in order that we might not disdain to receive from the Lord that which the Lord had received from a servant, should John have baptized the Lord alone?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if John had baptized the Lord alone, some would have thought that the baptism of John was more holy than that of Christ: as if Christ alone had been found worthy to be baptized with the baptism of John, but the human race with that of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, beloved brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With the baptism of Christ we have been baptized, and not only we, but the whole world, and this will continue to the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which of us can in any respect be compared with Christ, whose shoe’s latchet John declared himself unworthy to unloose?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the Christ, a man of such excellence, a man who is God, had been alone baptized with the baptism of John, what were men likely to say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What a baptism was that of John!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His was a great baptism, an ineffable sacrament; behold, Christ alone deserved to be baptized with the baptism of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus the baptism of the servant would appear greater than the baptism of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Others were also baptized with the baptism of John, that the baptism of John might not appear better than the baptism of Christ; but baptized also was the Lord, that through the Lord receiving the baptism of the servant, other servants might not disdain to receive the baptism of the Lord: for this end, then, was John sent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But did he know Christ, or did he not know Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he did not know Him, wherefore did He say, when Christ came to the river, “I have need to be baptized of Thee”? that is to say, I know who Thou art.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, he already knew Him, assuredly he knew Him when he saw the dove descending.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is evident that the dove did not descend upon the Lord until after He went up out of the water of baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Lord having been baptized, went up out of the water, and the heavens were opened, and he saw a dove descending on Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the dove descended after the baptism, and if, before the Lord was baptized, John said to Him, “Comest Thou to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee;” that is to say, before he knew Him to whom he said, “Comest Thou to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee;”—how then said he, “And I knew Him not: but He who sent me to baptize with water, the same said to me, Upon whom thou seest the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not an insignificant question, my brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you have seen the question, you have seen not a little; it remains that the Lord give the solution of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, however, I say, if you have seen the question, it is no small matter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, John is placed before your eyes, standing beside the river.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold John the Baptist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, the Lord comes, as yet to be baptized, not yet baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the voice of John, “Comest Thou to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, already he knew the Lord, by whom He wishes to be baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, having been baptized, goes up out of the water; the heavens are opened, the Spirit descends; then John knows Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then for the first time he knew Him, why did he say before, “I have need to be baptized of Thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if he did not then recognize Him for the first time, because he knew Him already, what is the meaning of what he said, “I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and abiding upon Him, as a dove, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, this question if solved today would oppress you, I do not doubt, for already have I spoken many words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But know that the question is of such a character that alone it is able to extinguish the party of Donatus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have said thus much, my beloved, in order to gain your attention, as is my wont; and also in order that you may pray for us, that the Lord may grant to us to speak what is suitable, and that you may be found worthy to receive what is suitable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the meantime, be pleased to defer the question for to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in the meantime, I say this briefly, until I give a fuller solution: Inquire peacefully, without quarreling, without contention, without altercations, without enmities; both seek by yourselves, and inquire of others, and say, “This question our bishop proposed to us to-day, and he will resolve it at a future time, if the Lord will.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whether it be resolved or not, reckon that I have propounded what appears to me of importance; for it does seem of considerable importance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John says, “I have need to be baptized of Thee,” as if he knew Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he did not know Him by whom he wished to be baptized, he spoke rashly when he said, “I have need to be baptized of Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he knew Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he knew Him, what is the meaning of the saying, “I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and abiding upon Him, as a dove, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall we say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That we do not know when the dove came?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lest perchance they take refuge in this, let the other evangelists be read, who have spoken of this matter more plainly, and we find most evidently that the dove then descended when the Lord came up out of the water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Upon Him baptized the heavens opened, and He saw the Spirit descending.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it was when He was already baptized that John knew Him, how saith he to Him, coming to baptism, “I have need to be baptized of Thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ponder this in the meantime with yourselves, confer upon it, treat of it, one with another.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord our God grant that before you hear it from me, the explanation may be revealed to some of you first.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, brethren, know this, that by means of the solution of this question, the allegation of the party of Donatus, if they have any sense of shame, will be silenced, and their mouths will be shut regarding the grace of baptism, a matter about which they raise mists to confuse the uninstructed, and spread nets for flying birds.
